steps.title: Identify the key signal about culture's function
steps.content: The decisive phrase is “to get the raw person to like that.” Culture doesn't simply accept what people naturally prefer; it actively works to make people adopt a standard of what is beautiful and graceful.

steps.title: Match culture's active role to the choices
steps.content: Choice D correctly identifies the function described: culture works to create a consensus about good taste, getting people to like and accept shared standards of what is beautiful and graceful. The other choices either shift the focus (A to disagreement, B to English reputation) or import unsupported elements (C to scientific methods). That matches choice D — D is correct.
